Name: Actual_IndividualIntervention_Config
Description: The configuration of an actual individual intervention to be distributed on the trigger. Selects a class for the intervention and configures the parameters specific for that intervention class.

Name: Actual_NodeIntervention_Config
Description: The configuration of the actual node-level intervention sought. This parameter selects a class for the intervention and configures the parameters specific for that intervention class.

Name: Blackout_Event_Trigger
Description: The event to broadcast if an intervention cannot be distributed due to the **Blackout_Period**. See :doc:`parameter-campaign-event-list` for possible values.

Name: Blackout_On_First_Occurrence
Description: If set to true (1), individuals will enter the blackout period after the first occurrence of an event in the **Trigger_Condition_List**.

Name: Blackout_Period
Description: After the initial intervention distribution, the time, in days, to wait before distributing the intervention again. If it cannot distribute due to the blackout period, it will broadcast the user-defined **Blackout_Event_Trigger**.

Name: Demographic_Coverage
Description: The fraction of individuals in the target demographic that will receive this intervention.

Name: Disqualifying_Properties
Description: A list of **NodeProperty** key:value pairs that cause an intervention to be aborted (persistent interventions will stop being distributed to nodes with these values). See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information. Generally used to control the flow of health care access. For example, to prevent the same individual from accessing health care via two different routes at the same time.

Name: Distribute_On_Return_Home
Description: When set to true (1), individuals will receive an intervention upon returning home if that intervention was originally distributed while the individual was away.

Name: Dont_Allow_Duplicates
Description: If an individual's container has an intervention, set to true (1) to prevent them from receiving another copy of the intervention. Supported by all intervention classes.

Name: Duration
Description: The number of days the NLHTIV will exist and listen for events/triggers. Note: Specifying a value of -1 results in indefinite persistence of the intervention.

Name: Intervention_Name
Description: The optional name used to refer to this intervention as a means to differentiate it from others that use the same class.

Name: New_Property_Value
Description: An optional **NodeProperty** key:value pair that will be assigned when the intervention is applied. See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information.

Name: Node_Property_Restrictions
Example:
The following example restricts the intervention to nodes that are urban and medium risk or rural and low risk.
[link](../json/campaign-nodelevelhealthtriggerediv_node_property_restrictions.json)
Description:
A list of the **NodeProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that nodes must have to be targeted by the intervention. See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information.

You can specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs with this parameter.

Name: Property_Restrictions
Description:
A list of the **IndividualProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that individuals must have to be targeted by this intervention. See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information.

To specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs, use **Property_Restrictions_Within_Node**. You cannot use both of these parameters in the same event coordinator.

Name: Property_Restrictions_Within_Node
Example:
The following example restricts the intervention to individuals who are urban and high risk or urban and medium risk.
[link](../json/campaign-nodelevelhealthtriggerediv_property_restrictions_within_node.json)
Description:
A list of the **IndividualProperty** key:value pairs, as defined in the demographics file, that individuals must have to be targeted by this intervention. See :ref:`demo-properties` parameters for more information.

This parameter allows you to specify AND and OR combinations of key:value pairs. You may specify individual property restrictions using either this parameter or **Property_Restrictions**, but not both.

Name: Target_Age_Max
Description: The upper end of ages targeted for an intervention, in years. Used when **Target_Demographic** is set to ExplicitAgeRanges or ExplicitAgeRangesAndGender.

Name: Target_Age_Min
Description: The lower end of ages targeted for an intervention, in years. Used when **Target_Demographic** is set to ExplicitAgeRanges or ExplicitAgeRangesAndGender.

Name: Target_Demographic
Description:
The target demographic group. Possible values are:

* Everyone
* ExplicitAgeRanges
* ExplicitAgeRangesAndGender
* ExplicitGender
* ExplicitDiseaseState

Name: Target_Gender
Description:
Specifies the gender restriction for the intervention. Possible values are:

* Male
* Female
* All

Name: Target_Residents_Only
Description: When set to true (1), the intervention is only distributed to individuals that began the simulation in the node (i.e. those that claim the node as their residence).

Name: Targeting_Config
Description: Be more selective of individuals by using the :doc:`parameter-campaign-targeting-config`.

Name: Trigger_Condition_List
Description: An array of events that will trigger an intervention. The events contained in the list can be built-in events (see :doc:`parameter-campaign-event-list` for possible events) or the custom events defined in **Custom_Individual_Events** in the simulation configuration file.
